race.name: Gsieser Tal Lauf
race.tagline: Forty-two kilometers through South Tyrol's snow-sure Gsies Valley — never cancelled since 1984, the Dolomites' most reliable date.

vitals.aid_stations: Feed stations with South Tyrolean refreshments

climate.description: The Gsies Valley at 1,300 meters altitude is one of the most snow-reliable valleys in the Alps. Temperatures range from -12°C to 2°C in late February. The high, enclosed valley position traps cold air and preserves snow quality. The race has never been cancelled due to lack of snow since its founding in 1984.
challenges: Cold morning starts in the shaded valley
Late-season sun can soften south-facing trail sections
Valley winds can funnel through narrow sections

course.primary: Out-and-back through the scenic Gsies Valley, surrounded by Dolomite peaks. The valley floor provides a natural corridor with moderate undulation. The enclosed alpine setting delivers consistent grooming on a well-maintained trail network.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails through alpine valley terrain
course.grooming: Excellent South Tyrolean standard — the valley floor provides an ideal grooming surface
features: Never been cancelled since 1984 — remarkable snow reliability
Enclosed Dolomite valley with dramatic mountain views
South Tyrolean village atmosphere with bilingual German-Italian culture
Traditional Tyrolean refreshments at feed stations

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Perfect snow reliability — never cancelled in over 40 years. Spectacular Dolomite scenery and strong South Tyrolean community atmosphere. The Euroloppet membership and solid field size anchor a well-rounded event. Moderate competitive depth reflects a predominantly recreational field.

history.summary: Founded in 1984, the Gsieser Tal Lauf has built its reputation on one extraordinary fact: it has never been cancelled. The Gsies Valley's position at 1,300 meters in the eastern Dolomites provides some of the most reliable natural snow conditions in the Alps. The race takes place in a German-speaking valley in South Tyrol, where Alpine farming traditions meet Italian flair. As a Euroloppet member, it draws participants from across central Europe seeking guaranteed snow and stunning mountain scenery.
notable_facts: Never been cancelled since its founding in 1984
Set in the Gsies Valley (Val Casies) at 1,300 meters altitude
South Tyrol's bilingual German-Italian culture adds unique character
The valley is one of the most snow-reliable locations in the entire Alps
Traditional Tyrolean hospitality at feed stations and finish area
